prompt: FIXED foreground subject: the woman and everything she wears and carries. Keep the warm backlight on her. Generate ONLY a new photographic background and ground plane. SCENE: a sun-warmed pale ochre limewashed wall in warm late-afternoon golden-hour light, the low sun behind and to the side of her creating the same warm backlight and soft glow already on her hair and shoulders — match the new scene's light direction and warmth to the light on her. A rustic weathered-wood bench edge enters low at camera-left; a closed sun-faded timber shutter sits camera-right; soft dappled olive-leaf shadow drifts across the wall. CRITICAL GROUNDING: a clear soft-edged contact shadow beneath her boots plus her own long soft shadow cast across the ground away from the low sun, so she is firmly planted, never floating. TONAL UNITY: wall, ground and all shadows in the same warm golden register as the light on her — one single exposure, warm filmic grade. Part of a set of the same woman by the same limewashed wall; keep wall colour and grade consistent. FRAMING: vertical portrait ~2:3, three-quarter length, a step closer so the wall edge and her resting hand read clearly.
